en:
Many people in industry and the Services, who have practical experience of noise, regard any investigation of its effects as a waste of time.
They are not prepared even to admit the possibility that noise affects people in any way.
On the other hand, those who dislike noise will sometimes use most inadequate evidence to support their pleas for a quieter society.
This is a pity, because noise abatement really is a good cause, and it is likely to be discredited if it gets associated with bad science.
One allegation often made is that noise produces mental illness.
The snag in such anecdotes is that one cannot distinguish cause from effect: was the noise a cause of the illness, or were complaints about noise merely a symptom?
What is needed is a study of large numbers of people living under noisy conditions, to discover whether they are mentally ill more often than other people are.
Even large-scale surveys, such as those conducted by the United States Navy on aircraft carriers, have failed to settle the question once and for all.
cn:
许多在工业部门和军队服役、对噪音有实际经验的人，认为研究这一问题纯属浪费时间。
他们甚至不愿承认噪音可能对人产生任何影响。
另一方面，厌恶噪音的人有时会用极不充分的证据来支持他们关于更安静社会的呼吁。
这令人遗憾，因为降低噪音确是一桩好事，若与拙劣的科学扯在一起，很可能名誉扫地。
一种常见的说法是噪音会导致精神疾病。
这类轶事的症结在于无法区分因果：噪音是致病原因，还是对噪音的抱怨只是症状？
需要的是对大量生活在嘈杂环境中的人进行研究，以弄清他们是否比其他人更易患精神疾病。
即便是美国海军在航空母舰上进行的大规模调查，也未能一劳永逸地解决这一问题。

en:
It is animals and plants which lived in or near water whose remains are most likely to be preserved.
The geological record reveals that life has evolved through countless ages, leaving traces buried in sediments and rock formations.
Remains that are exposed to air and decay quickly vanish, unless they are buried before decomposition sets in.
Fossils preserved in ancient lake beds tell us far more than scattered bones on open plains.
Until recently, scientists could only guess at how the earth looked millions of years ago.
The study of strata in which fossils are embedded has transformed our picture of the planet's distant past.
Creatures that once flourished in shallow seas have left impressions that endure for geological ages.
To understand the present world, we must read the story written in stone by vanished ecosystems.
cn:
正是生活在水中或水边的动植物，其遗骸最易保存下来。
地质记录揭示，生命历经无数世代演化，在沉积物和岩层中留下痕迹。
暴露于空气并腐烂的遗骸很快消失，除非在分解开始之前被掩埋。
保存在古老湖床中的化石，比开阔平原上散落的骨骼更能说明问题。
直到不久前，科学家还只能猜测数百万年前地球的模样。
对含化石地层的研究，彻底改变了我们对地球远古面貌的认识。
曾在浅海中繁盛的生物，留下了绵延地质年代的印痕。
要理解当今的世界，必须解读那些由消逝的生态系统铭刻在石头上的故事。

en:
The mighty Vasa, which was intended to rule the Baltic, had been laboured over for three years before she was launched at Stockholm.
She presented a more majestic spectacle than Stockholmers had ever seen, with triple gun-decks mounting sixty-four bronze cannon.
In that first glorious hour, the imperial flagship seemed invincible.
As the wind freshened, there came a sudden squall and the ship made a strange movement, listing to port.
The Ordnance officer ordered all the port cannon to be heaved to starboard to counteract the list, but the steepening angle of the decks only increased.
Cargo, ballast, ammunition and four hundred people went sliding down to the port side of the steeply listing ship.
The lower gun-ports were now below water, and the inrush of sea water sealed the ship's fate.
Within minutes, the Vasa, which had been built to display Sweden's naval might, lay at the bottom of the harbour.
cn:
威力无比的「瓦萨」号本欲称霸波罗的海，在斯德哥尔摩下水之前，工匠们已为之劳作了三年。
她配备三层炮甲板、六十四门青铜加农炮，其雄伟景象斯德哥尔摩人前所未见。
在最初那光辉的一刻，这艘帝国旗舰似乎所向无敌。
风势加剧时突然袭来一阵暴风，船身奇怪地倾斜，向左舷倾侧。
军械官下令将左舷大炮全部移至右舷以抵消倾斜，但甲板倾角却愈发陡峭。
货物、压舱物、弹药和四百人滑向急剧倾斜船只的左舷一侧。
下层炮眼已没入水下，海水涌入注定了这艘船的命运。
几分钟之内，为展示瑞典海军威力而建造的瓦萨号便沉入了港底。

en:
The majority of out-patients attending a physician feel they have not received adequate treatment unless they can take home some tangible remedy.
What they demand is a bottle of medicine, a box of pills, or at least a small jar of ointment.
There is no quicker way of disposing of patients than by prescribing some drug which they can carry away with them.
Nor is it only the ignorant and ill-educated who place such faith in the curative properties of the medicine bottle.
Although our scepticism about many remedies has grown, our confidence in a tangible prescription remains remarkably persistent.
Thomas Carlyle once visited a friend carrying in his pocket what remained of a bottle formerly prescribed for his wife's minor indisposition.
Patients who expect instant cures are often disappointed when doctors offer only advice and reassurance.
Every patient demands to be cured at no inconvenience to himself — beyond putting up for a moment with a disgusting taste.
cn:
大多数门诊病人觉得，除非能带回家的有形药物，否则便未得到充分治疗。
他们所要的是一瓶药、一盒药丸，或至少一小罐药膏。
没有比开些可带走的药更快打发病人的办法了。
迷信药瓶疗效的并非只有无知少识之人。
尽管我们对许多疗法的怀疑日增，对有形处方的信心却依然根深蒂固。
托马斯·卡莱尔曾揣着一瓶曾为其妻轻微不适而开的余药去看望朋友。
期望立即痊愈的病人，往往在医生只给建议与安慰时大失所望。
每位病人都要求在不给自己带来不便的情况下被治愈——至多忍受一下难吃的味道。

en:
Christopher Cockerell, who had turned from boat-building to research, suggested an idea on which he had been working for many years.
His hovercraft rides on a cushion of air trapped inside a flexible rubber skirt that rings the hull.
By this means, friction with the ground or water is greatly reduced.
The first tests on the Solent in 1959 caused a sensation among engineers and journalists alike.
The fact that a craft could skim over land and water on a mere cushion of air seemed almost miraculous.
Cockerell faced years of scepticism before the hovercraft won official support and funding.
Ships that depend on displacement must push water aside; hovercraft that ride on air move with far less resistance.
The invention opened possibilities for rapid transport across marshes, mud flats and shallow coastal waters.
cn:
曾从造船转向研究的克里斯托弗·科克雷尔，提出了一个他已酝酿多年的构想。
他的气垫船依靠围在船体外的柔性橡胶裙内形成的气垫行驶。
借此，与地面或水面的摩擦力大为减小。
一九五九年在索伦特海峡的首次试验在工程师和记者中引起轰动。
一种交通工具能仅凭气垫在陆地和水面滑行，这似乎近乎奇迹。
科克雷尔历经多年质疑，气垫船才获得官方支持与资助。
依靠排水量航行的船须推开海水；乘气垫行驶的船阻力小得多。
这项发明为穿越沼泽、泥滩和浅海沿岸水域的快速交通开辟了可能。

en:
For centuries, the floor of the deep ocean remained as mysterious as the surface of the moon.
It was not until 1869 that a series of deep soundings was obtained in the Atlantic by dredging.
Physicists and geologists soon realised that the topography of the ocean floor was far more rugged than anyone had imagined.
Mountain ranges hidden beneath the waves rival the highest peaks on land.
Sediments that settle on the sea-floor preserve a record of climatic and geological change.
The crust beneath the oceans, which geologists had long underestimated, shapes the movement of continents.
Explorers who brave the crushing pressure of the depths bring back evidence that transforms our picture of the earth.
To understand the planet fully, we must descend into the abyss where light never falls.
cn:
数个世纪以来，深海海底仍像月球表面一样神秘莫测。
直到一八六九年，才通过挖掘在大西洋取得一系列深海测深数据。
物理学家和地质学家很快意识到，海底地形远比任何人想象的更为崎岖。
隐藏在波浪下的山脉可与陆地最高峰相媲美。
沉积在海床上的沉积物保存着气候与地质变迁的记录。
地质学家长期低估的海洋地壳，左右着大陆的运动。
勇闯深海巨大压力的探险者带回的证据，改变了我们对地球的认识。
要全面了解这个星球，必须潜入光线永不到达的深渊。

en:
Appreciation of sculpture depends upon the ability to respond to form in three dimensions.
Though we may admire a painting at a glance, sculpture demands that we walk around it, viewing it from many angles.
The sculptor who works in stone or bronze thinks in terms of mass, volume and shadow.
Unlike the painter, who creates an illusion on a flat surface, the sculptor shapes material that occupies real space.
Children who learn to model clay early often develop a natural sensitivity to three-dimensional form.
The greatest works invite us to circle them, discovering new relationships between curve and plane at every step.
Sculpture, which speaks to the sense of touch as well as to the eye, offers an experience no other art can duplicate.
To appreciate it fully, we must learn to see with our bodies as well as with our minds.
cn:
欣赏雕塑取决于对三维形态的感知能力。
我们或许一眼便能欣赏画作，雕塑却要求我们绕行观赏，从多角度审视。
从事石雕或青铜创作的雕塑家，以体量、体积和阴影来思考。
与在平面上制造幻觉的画家不同，雕塑家塑造占据真实空间的材料。
自幼学习捏塑粘土的儿童，往往对三维形态产生天然的敏感。
最伟大的作品邀请我们环绕而行，每一步都发现曲线与平面之间新的关系。
雕塑诉诸触觉与视觉，提供其他艺术无法复制的体验。
要真正欣赏它，必须学会用身体去观看，而不仅用头脑。

en:
Galileo was, above all, a man who experimented and who put his questions to nature instead of to the ancients.
When he dropped weights from the Leaning Tower of Pisa, he challenged centuries of Aristotelian authority.
He observed the heavens through a telescope, discovering moons that revolved around Jupiter and phases that proved Venus orbits the sun.
Scientists who remember this seldom forget that progress depends on direct observation rather than inherited dogma.
If we recall how fiercely the establishment opposed him, we understand why his story still inspires researchers today.
Galileo's methods, which were once condemned by the Inquisition, have become the foundation of modern physical science.
He was forced to recant his views, yet the earth still moves around the sun.
To ask nature the right questions, Galileo taught us, is the first duty of every true scientist.
cn:
伽利略首先是实验者，他向自然而非古人提问。
他从比萨斜塔上抛下重物，挑战了延续数世纪的亚里士多德权威。
他用望远镜观测天空，发现绕木星运转的卫星，以及证明金星绕日运行的盈亏现象。
记得这一点的科学家很少忘记：进步依赖直接观察，而非沿袭的教条。
若回想当权派曾如何激烈反对他，便不难理解他的故事至今仍激励着研究者。
曾被宗教法庭谴责的伽利略方法，已成为现代物理学的基础。
他被迫收回言论，但地球依然绕太阳运转。
伽利略教导我们：向自然提出正确的问题，是每个真正科学家的首要职责。

en:
Education is often compared unfavourably with the informal learning that takes place in tribal societies without books or schools.
If our educational system were fashioned after its bookless past, we would have the most democratic form of college imaginable.
In jungles and on savannahs, there is no juvenile delinquency of the kind that troubles modern cities.
Children who grow up in such communities master complex skills through imitation and direct experience, not through compulsory attendance.
There are no illiterates among peoples without a written script, while compulsory schooling became law in Germany as early as 1642.
We have imprisoned ourselves within systems that separate knowledge from life.
Tribes that remain close to nature educate their young by involving them in every necessary task.
Perhaps the greatest lesson is that formal schooling is only one path — and not always the best — to wisdom.
cn:
正规教育常与没有书本和学校的部落社会中的非正式学习相比，显得逊色。
若我们的教育体系仿效其无书的过去，我们将拥有可以想象的最民主的学院形式。
丛林与大草原中没有困扰现代城市的那种青少年犯罪。
在此类社群中长大的儿童，通过模仿和直接体验掌握复杂技能，而非靠义务教育。
没有文字的民族中没有文盲，而德国早在1642年就将义务教育定为法律。
我们把自己囚禁在将知识与生命割裂的体制之中。
贴近自然的部落让年轻人参与一切必要劳作，以此教育他们。
或许最大的启示是：正规学校教育只是通往智慧的一条路——而且并非总是最好的一条。

en:
It is the adolescent who, for the first time, sees through the pretensions of adult life and resents the hypocrisy he detects.
If parents were prepared for this reaction, they would not be so deeply hurt when their children criticize them.
The young person who once accepted every parental verdict now questions motives that were never questioned before.
Were parents less convinced of their own infallibility, they might welcome honest disagreement as a sign of growing maturity.
Never before in history have so many adolescents enjoyed such freedom, yet never have so many felt so misunderstood.
Parents who cling to Victorian attitudes of unreasoning authority often drive their children into hostile silence.
Adolescence is a period of disillusionment as well as growth: ideals fade, and the world proves less perfect than childhood promised.
Only when both sides acknowledge the pain of this transition can the family weather the storm without lasting damage.
cn:
正是青少年第一次看穿成人生活的伪装，并怨恨所察觉的虚伪。
若父母对这种反应有所准备，子女批评他们时便不会如此受伤。
曾经接受一切父母裁决的年轻人，如今质疑从未被质疑过的动机。
父母若不那么确信自己一贯正确，或许会把坦诚的分歧视为成熟的标志。
历史上从未有如此多的青少年享有自由，却也从未有如此多的人感到被误解。
固守维多利亚式非理性权威态度的父母，往往把子女逼入敌意的沉默。
青春期既是幻灭也是成长的时期：理想褪色，世界不如童年所许诺的那般完美。
唯有双方都承认这一过渡的痛苦，家庭才能经受风暴而不留长久创伤。

en:
Because the Moon has only an eightieth of the Earth's mass, it requires ninety-seven per cent less energy to travel from the lunar surface to Earth orbit than from Earth's surface to the same point.
The Moon, which is the world most like our own, may become the hub of future space exploration.
From a lunar base, spacecraft can be launched with far less fuel, making journeys to Mars and beyond economically conceivable.
Apollo astronauts, who walked on the dust of another world, proved that human beings can survive beyond the atmosphere.
Oxygen, water and minerals frozen in lunar shadows could sustain colonies that live off the land.
Engineers who design future missions must account for the vast chasm between terrestrial and extraterrestrial conditions.
The fascination of space lies not only in distance but in the challenge of building a permanent foothold beyond Earth.
Our odyssey has barely begun: each small step on the Moon points toward a longer journey among the stars.
cn:
由于月球质量仅为地球的八十分之一，从月球表面到地球轨道所需的能量比从地球表面到同一位置少百分之九十七。
月球是与地球最相似的天体，可能成为未来太空探索的中心。
从月球基地发射航天器所需燃料少得多，使前往火星及更远处的旅程在经济上成为可能。
在另一世界尘土上行走的阿波罗宇航员，证明了人类可以在大气层外生存。
冻结在月球阴影中的氧气、水和矿物，可维持自给自足的殖民地。
设计未来任务的工程师必须考虑地球与地外环境之间的巨大鸿沟。
太空的魅力不仅在于距离，更在于在地球之外建立永久立足点的挑战。
我们的太空漫游才刚刚开始：在月球上的每一小步，都指向群星间更漫长的旅程。

en:
The administrative overheads of a nation are analogous to the management costs of a business enterprise.
If the people in a country can be trusted to act loyally and responsibly, government overheads will be low.
A nation that is essentially disunited must maintain large standing armies of bureaucrats and police officers.
The more disunited a people, the greater the administrative machinery needed to keep them in order.
Costs which could be spent on education and development are diverted to maintaining authority by force.
To the extent that citizens participate willingly in public life, the state need not exert constant surveillance.
Countries where mutual trust prevails spend correspondingly less on law enforcement and internal control.
The price of disunity, measured not in bombs but in bureaucracy, is one that every society eventually pays.
cn:
一个国家的行政开支，类似于企业的管理费用。
若国民可以信赖、能忠诚负责地行事，政府开支便会保持低位。
本质上分裂的国家必须维持大批官僚和警察。
人民越分裂，维持秩序所需的行政机器就越庞大。
本可用于教育和发展的事业经费，被转用于以武力维持权威。
在公民自愿参与公共生活的程度上，国家无需施加持续监视。
互相信任的国家在执法与内部管控上的花费相应更少。
分裂的代价——不以炸弹衡量，而以官僚体制衡量——是每个社会最终都要付出的。
